class_schedule.topic: Capstone Project Selection and Team Formation
learning_objectives: Select a real-world project scenario for the capstone
Form cross-functional teams and establish team charters
Define the capstone deliverables and assessment criteria

lecture_notes.introduction: The capstone integrates everything you have learned. You will manage a realistic project from initiation through execution planning, demonstrating mastery of all PM knowledge areas.
key_concepts: Project selection criteria: complexity, multiple stakeholders, cross-domain requirements
Team formation: diverse skills, complementary strengths, assigned roles
Team charter: communication norms, decision-making process, conflict resolution, accountability
Capstone deliverables: project charter, full PM plan, risk assessment, stakeholder engagement, performance framework
Assessment: peer evaluation, sponsor review, faculty panel presentation
lecture_notes.demonstration: Reviewing past capstone project examples and discussing what made top projects exceptional

review.value: Choose a project complex enough to demonstrate all PM skills but scoped realistically for a 15-week course
The first deliverable; sets the foundation for how the team will work together throughout the semester

class_schedule.topic: Project Initiation and Business Case Development
learning_objectives: Develop a business case with financial justification
Draft the project charter with all required components
Conduct initial stakeholder identification and analysis

lecture_notes.introduction: Initiation sets the direction. A strong business case and charter are the foundation for everything that follows.
key_concepts: Business case: problem/opportunity, proposed solution, cost-benefit analysis, alternatives considered
Financial justification: NPV, ROI, payback period, IRR
Charter components: purpose, objectives, scope, milestones, budget, PM authority, stakeholders
Stakeholder identification: brainstorming, organizational analysis, expert judgment
Power-interest grid: initial stakeholder classification and engagement strategy
lecture_notes.demonstration: Faculty review of a sample business case and charter with feedback on common weaknesses

review.value: Must answer: why should the organization invest in this project? What happens if we don't?
The charter is the PM's license to operate; vague charters lead to scope disputes and authority gaps later

class_schedule.topic: Comprehensive Planning Workshop I: Scope and Schedule
learning_objectives: Develop a detailed WBS with work packages
Build a CPM schedule with resource assignments

lecture_notes.introduction: Planning workshop sessions apply all techniques from PM102 and beyond to your capstone project.
key_concepts: WBS development: top-down decomposition to work package level
Activity definition and sequencing with PDM
Duration estimation using PERT three-point estimates
Critical path analysis and float calculations
Schedule optimization: crashing and fast-tracking where needed
lecture_notes.demonstration: Faculty-facilitated work session where teams build their WBS and begin scheduling

review.value: The WBS drives the schedule, budget, risk analysis, and resource plan; quality here cascades through all other plans
Teams review each other's WBS for completeness and logic; external perspective catches blind spots

class_schedule.topic: Comprehensive Planning Workshop II: Cost, Risk, and Quality
learning_objectives: Develop a bottom-up cost estimate and cost baseline
Create a comprehensive risk register with response plans
Design a quality management plan with metrics

lecture_notes.introduction: Cost, risk, and quality plans build on the scope and schedule work. Each informs and constrains the others.
key_concepts: Bottom-up cost estimate: labor, materials, equipment, overhead, contingency, management reserve
Cost baseline as S-curve: time-phased spending plan
Risk identification: brainstorming, checklists, SWOT, expert interviews
Qualitative and quantitative risk analysis for top risks
Quality management plan: standards, metrics, inspections, audits
lecture_notes.demonstration: Faculty-facilitated work session for cost estimation, risk identification, and quality planning

review.value: EMV analysis from risk register feeds into contingency reserve; the two plans must be connected
Define how you'll know the project deliverables meet requirements; metrics must be measurable and tied to acceptance criteria

class_schedule.topic: Communication, Stakeholder, and Change Management Plans
learning_objectives: Develop integrated communication and stakeholder engagement plans
Create a change management strategy for project adoption
Design the performance measurement framework for the project

lecture_notes.introduction: The people-focused plans ensure that technical delivery translates into actual adoption and benefit realization.
key_concepts: Communication matrix: stakeholder information needs mapped to channels and frequency
Stakeholder engagement assessment: current vs. desired levels with strategies
Change management integration: ADKAR analysis for key stakeholder groups
Performance measurement framework: EVM setup, KPIs, reporting templates
Integrated project management plan: combining all subsidiary plans
lecture_notes.demonstration: Faculty review of teams' draft communication and stakeholder plans with peer feedback

review.value: All subsidiary plans must reference each other; the risk register informs communication; the stakeholder plan drives change management
The integrated PM plan should have no gaps; every process group and knowledge area should be addressed

learning_objectives: Present the complete project management plan to a faculty panel
Demonstrate integration across all knowledge areas
readings: Review all course materials and capstone deliverables

lecture_notes.introduction: The capstone presentation simulates presenting to a project steering committee. You must demonstrate mastery and handle questions confidently.
key_concepts: Presentation structure: business case, approach, key risks, schedule, budget, team, ask
Executive communication: concise, decision-focused, bottom-line-up-front
Defense preparation: anticipate tough questions about assumptions, risks, and alternatives
Peer evaluation: assessing team contributions objectively
Lessons learned: reflection on what the team learned about PM and teamwork
lecture_notes.demonstration: Faculty panel presentations with Q&A, scoring, and feedback

review.value: Clear structure, professional delivery, confident Q&A responses, demonstrated integration of all PM knowledge
Completeness, internal consistency, realistic assumptions, and professional documentation

major_assignments.title: Capstone Project Management Plan
major_assignments.description: Develop a complete, integrated project management plan for a complex project, including all subsidiary plans, presented to a faculty panel simulating a steering committee.
objectives: Demonstrate mastery of all PM knowledge areas in an integrated plan
Produce professional-quality deliverables suitable for real-world use
Present and defend the plan to a panel of evaluators
Work effectively as a team, demonstrating PM leadership skills
deliverables: Scope management plan with WBS and WBS dictionary
Schedule management plan with CPM network and Gantt chart
Cost management plan with bottom-up estimate and S-curve baseline
Risk management plan with risk register (20+ risks), qualitative and quantitative analysis
Quality management plan with metrics and inspection procedures
Communication management plan with stakeholder engagement assessment
Change management strategy with ADKAR analysis
Performance measurement framework with EVM setup
Executive presentation (15-20 minutes) with Q&A defense
Peer evaluation and individual reflection (1-2 pages each)

criteria: Business case is compelling with sound financial justification
Charter is complete and professionally formatted
Stakeholder analysis is thorough and actionable
WBS is complete, follows 100% rule, and has clear work packages
Schedule is realistic with correct CPM logic and resource assignments
Cost estimate is detailed, realistic, and properly includes reserves
All three baselines are established and consistent with each other
Risk register has 20+ risks with proper analysis and response plans
Quality plan has measurable metrics and defined inspection procedures
Communication and stakeholder plans are tailored to specific stakeholder needs
All subsidiary plans reference and support each other
Change management strategy uses ADKAR or equivalent framework
Performance measurement framework is complete and practical
Presentation is clear, professional, and well-structured
Team demonstrates deep understanding of the plan, not just surface knowledge
Q&A responses are confident, accurate, and demonstrate integration thinking
Peer evaluations indicate equitable contribution
Individual reflection demonstrates genuine learning and self-awareness
Team dynamics were constructive and professional
Complete and integrated project management plan
Professional quality suitable for real-world use
Strong presentation and confident defense
Effective teamwork with individual accountability

capstone_project.description: Complete project management plan, presentation, and defense

peer_evaluation.description: Team contribution as evaluated by peers

individual_reflection.description: Personal reflection on learning and professional development

class_participation.description: Active participation in workshops, peer reviews, and in-class work sessions

course_policies.attendance: Attendance is mandatory for all sessions. This is a workshop-based course where every session contributes to the capstone. More than 2 absences result in a grade reduction.
course_policies.late_submissions: Capstone milestones have firm deadlines aligned with workshop sessions. Late milestones impact the entire team and are penalized at 15% per day.
course_policies.collaboration: The capstone is a team project. All team members must contribute meaningfully. Free-riding will be identified through peer evaluation and result in individual grade adjustment.
course_policies.academic_integrity: All work must be original. The plan must be for the assigned scenario, not adapted from real-world plans found online.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Microsoft Project, spreadsheet application, presentation software. All tools used throughout the PM program are expected to be applied.
